# Develop brand voice → generate a BrandGPT system prompt

Setup: Maya runs a 7-figure DTC + Amazon apparel brand. Her new sustainable-apparel launch
(ASIN B0APP0002) has an Avatar 2.0 + Brand Canvas on record but no codified voice and no
AI assistant. No reviews yet (fresh launch). She wants a tight voice spec and a paste-ready
BrandGPT system prompt — drafted, not a worksheet to fill in.

---

**Maya (User):** B0APP0002, sustainable apparel, new launch, zero reviews. Avatar (av_B0APP0002 — Conscious-Consumer, US women 28–42, anti-greenwashing, pays $48/tee) and the 8-statement canvas are done. I need two things: a voice spec I can hand a copywriter, and a system prompt I can paste into a custom GPT so my AI stops sounding generic. I know the four pillars and the four elements — don't re-teach me. Draft both.

**Coach:** On it — voice spec, then a paste-ready BrandGPT prompt. One framing only, no lecture: voice is "more than just words… the essence of how it communicates," and it's the lever for "establishing trust, evoking emotion, and ensuring consistency" — so I'll lock the emotional spine first, then both deliverables run off it. Pulling your on-record context as the input, not starting blank.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/00-brand-voice-overview.md⟧

⟦tool: run_conversation — input {message: "<Maya's request: voice spec + BrandGPT prompt for B0APP0002>", avatar_context: "av_B0APP0002 sustainable apparel; 8-statement canvas on record"}⟧
→ extracted_context: { spine: "trust through verifiable honesty", category: "everyday sustainable apparel", audience: "eco-conscious US women 28–42", differentiator: "verifiable sourcing, no greenwashing, made-to-last", canvas_personality: "honest, grounded, quietly confident, exacting — the straight-talking insider who shows the receipts" }

**Maya:** Spine's right — trust through verifiable honesty. That's the canvas. Don't make me pick a tone from a menu. Give me the tone, the language rules, the do/don't. And it has to survive a returns email, not just a hero headline.

**Coach:** Agreed, and that's the Authentic test, not an extra — the voice has to be "consistent across all platforms," same essence "whether they encounter it through an ad, a website, or a customer service representative," so I'm writing it to hold in a returns email by construction.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/01-voice-through-the-four-pillars/03-authentic-voice.md⟧

Here's the voice run through the four pillars first (your strategy), then assembled into the four elements (the craft) — I'll fill the boxes for you:

- **Insight-Driven** — built from how *your* customer wants to be spoken to, not what the brand wants to say: mirror her language and validate the emotion she's actually feeling (the distrust, the "is this actually sustainable?" fatigue) before any pitch. Since you have zero reviews yet, the language pull comes from the avatar + your founding-why, not invented quotes.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/01-voice-through-the-four-pillars/00-insight-driven-voice.md⟧
- **Distinctive** — pick the archetype and own the gap. From your canvas personality, the archetype is **straight-talking insider who shows the receipts** — warm-but-no-fluff, not the playful/witty or purely authoritative ends. The competitor gap nobody occupies: *proof-first* in a category that leans aspirational-vague. And the UVP ("verifiable, no greenwashing") gets infused into the tone — clear and uncluttered, because you champion honesty, not spectacle.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/01-voice-through-the-four-pillars/01-distinctive-voice.md⟧
- **Empathetic** — name the pain point *before* the solution: greenwashing fatigue, the guilt/doubt of not knowing. Validation mode here is **reassurance** (you remove doubt), with a thread of **shared ambition** (wear better without compromise) — not cheerleading.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/01-voice-through-the-four-pillars/02-empathetic-voice.md⟧

**Maya:** Good. Reassurance, not cheerleading — correct. Now the elements. And give me a real do/don't, not "be authentic."

**Coach:** Here are all four elements, aligned to the one emotion (trust-through-honesty) so the experience reads as "aspiration, identity, and belonging," not just a tee — that alignment is what "subtly guides them toward purchase":   ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/02-the-four-elements-of-voice/00-cohesive-voice-overview.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/03-aligning-voice-with-emotional-triggers.md⟧

- **Tone** (the emotional signature, named in adjectives): **plain-spoken, evidence-first, warm-but-exacting.** On the book's spectrum that's nearer Innocent's friendly register than Rolex's authoritative one — but earned by proof, not charm. Like the TAG Heuer model, the tone only lands if it's backed by real proof; for you the "F1 partnership" equivalent is your published sourcing.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/02-the-four-elements-of-voice/01-tone-of-voice.md⟧
- **Language** (taglines + copy that reinforce identity): translate the function ("organic tee") into the emotional one ("wear it without second-guessing") — active, aspirational, speaks to who she wants to *become* (someone who no longer has to ask "is this real?"). The "Don't Crack Under Pressure" move for you: turn a spec into a feeling.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/02-the-four-elements-of-voice/02-language.md⟧
- **Design + Imagery** (visual extension — flagged, your designer owns the build): color/type/layout must carry the same message as the words; lean clean/minimal so focus stays on the proof (color psychology deliberately, not decoration). I'll note it in the spec and stop there — you didn't ask me to design.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/02-the-four-elements-of-voice/03-design-visual-language.md⟧

Do / Don't (the operational rules):
- **Do:** claim, then proof — every claim followed by the receipt. Name the doubt before the product. Same voice in a hero headline and a returns email.
- **Don't:** exaggerate or use industry jargon ("sustainable" as a bare badge, "eco-friendly," "clean") — strip it for "clarity and honesty"; never let a "sales mode" switch show.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/01-voice-through-the-four-pillars/03-authentic-voice.md⟧

**Maya:** That's the spec. Now I need taglines / a signature line for it — and then the GPT prompt. Don't give me one safe option.

**Coach:** Generating distinct signature lines off the locked voice — multiple options, not one, and no parroting your inputs back.   ⟦tool: generate_signature⟧

⟦tool: generate_signature — input {avatar_id: "av_B0APP0002", conversation: ["<run_conversation transcript: spine + voice spec>"], voice_spec: "<tone+language+do/don't above>"}⟧
→ 4 distinct signature options returned (frozen no-parroting prompt; accepted the conversation[] transcript as input — fields/reviews empty is fine):
  1. "Proof over promises."
  2. "Sustainable basics you never have to second-guess."
  3. "We show the receipts."
  4. "Wear it without the doubt."
⟦iv-os: log_asset — chosen signature persisted against B0APP0002⟧ *(mocked: no canon/asset data invented by coach; save routes through IV-OS, not a brand-coach store)*

**Maya:** Going with "Proof over promises" as the line, "We show the receipts" as the supporting one. Now build the GPT prompt around all of it.

**Coach:** Building it. The system prompt is generated from your completed brand inputs — that's the rule: the AI is only as on-brand as the structured doc behind it, so I'm assembling the worksheet inputs (positioning, audience, voice, key phrases) into the prompt rather than free-writing one. Feeding the canvas + voice spec + signature as the knowledge so every response stays Insight-Driven, Distinctive, Empathetic, Authentic.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/00-custom-gpt/01-idea-ai-training-workflow/00-prepare-the-worksheet.md⟧

⟦tool: export_strategy_doc — input {avatar_id: "av_B0APP0002", brand_context: "<voice spec + signature + canvas spine>", section: "brandgpt_system_instructions", format: "doc"}⟧
→ strategy doc generated: a brand-voice spec + a complete BrandGPT system-instructions prompt following the Ch7 section scaffold (Role & Purpose → Guiding Principles → Brand Positioning → Target Audience → Core Product Benefits → System Behavior → Example Interaction → Brand Voice & Personality → Compliance → Additional Features → How to Handle User Queries → Final Instructions); idempotent per avatar snapshot.
⟦iv-os: log_asset — voice spec + BrandGPT prompt linked to B0APP0002⟧ *(mocked)*

**Coach:** Both drafted below. The prompt keeps every section heading from the book's template as a checklist — including Compliance with a "what NOT to say" bad/good pair, which isn't optional: it's how the AI stays honest under pressure. Paste it into your custom GPT alongside the canvas as the uploaded knowledge file. Edit-ready.

### Artifact produced

#### Part A — Brand-Voice Spec (B0APP0002, sustainable apparel) — DRAFT, edit-ready

| Layer | Spec (drafted for you) | Grounding |
|---|---|---|
| **Emotional spine** | Trust through verifiable honesty — own ONE emotion across every touchpoint so the experience reads as aspiration/identity/belonging, guiding to purchase. | `03-aligning-voice-with-emotional-triggers.md` |
| **Pillar — Insight-Driven** | Mirror her language + validate the emotion (greenwashing fatigue, "is this actually sustainable?") before the pitch. No invented review quotes (zero reviews). | `00-insight-driven-voice.md` |
| **Pillar — Distinctive** | Archetype: straight-talking insider who shows the receipts. Own the *proof-first* gap; infuse UVP (verifiable, no greenwashing) → clear, uncluttered tone. | `01-distinctive-voice.md` |
| **Pillar — Empathetic** | Pain before solution; validation mode = **reassurance** + thread of **shared ambition**; never cheerlead. | `02-empathetic-voice.md` |
| **Pillar — Authentic** | Strip exaggeration + jargon; trace to mission; same voice in ad, site, and a returns email — no "sales mode" switch. | `03-authentic-voice.md` |
| **Element — Tone** | Plain-spoken, evidence-first, warm-but-exacting (Innocent end of the spectrum, earned by proof à la TAG Heuer's proof-backed tone). | `01-tone-of-voice.md` |
| **Element — Language** | Function → feeling ("organic tee" → "wear it without second-guessing"); active, aspirational; speaks to who she becomes. | `02-language.md` |
| **Element — Design + Imagery** | Visual must carry the same message; clean/minimal, deliberate color psychology — *designer owns the build* (flagged, not designed here). | `03-design-visual-language.md` |
| **Signature line** | Primary: **"Proof over promises."** Supporting: **"We show the receipts."** | generate_signature output |
| **Do / Don't** | DO: claim→proof; name the doubt first; one voice headline-to-returns-email. DON'T: bare "sustainable/eco/clean" badges; jargon; visible sales-mode switch. | `03-authentic-voice.md` |

#### Part B — BrandGPT System-Instructions Prompt (paste-ready)

```
B0APP0002 BrandGPT  —  [YourBrand] Sustainable Basics

Role & Purpose:
You are [YourBrand]'s GPT, a brand assistant and AI-powered expert for [YourBrand]
sustainable everyday apparel. Your role is to provide accurate, on-brand, and
trustworthy information about our verifiably-sourced organic basics, and to generate
on-brand content. You guide eco-conscious shoppers who are tired of greenwashing,
helping them choose pieces they can wear without second-guessing. You have been
provided with our IDEA Brand Canvas and Brand-Voice Spec to enable accurate,
on-brand answers.

Guiding Principles:
- Insight-Driven: Root every response in the customer's real motivation (distrust of
  greenwashed basics) and validate the emotion before recommending.
- Distinctive: Maintain the "straight-talking insider who shows the receipts" voice
  that sets us apart from aspirational-vague competitors.
- Empathetic: Lead with reassurance — name the doubt before the solution; never cheerlead.
- Authentic: Claim, then proof. Never exaggerate, never use bare "sustainable/eco/clean"
  badges, never make misleading environmental claims.

Brand Positioning: [YourBrand]
For eco-conscious women who are tired of greenwashed basics, [YourBrand] makes
verifiably-sourced everyday apparel that's built to last — so they can trust what they
wear and stop second-guessing "sustainable." Every claim is published with its proof;
nothing is sold on a leaf logo. Signature: "Proof over promises."

Target Audience:
- Eco-conscious women, US, ~28–42, values-driven (identity-signaling, not price-shopping).
- Shoppers burned by greenwashed or quietly-fast-fashion "sustainable" basics.
- People who want a plain organic tee they can trust and wear daily.

Core Product Benefits:
- Verifiable Sourcing – every sustainability claim is published with its receipt.
- Made to Last – built to outlast trend cycles, not a season.
- No Greenwashing – plain language, no badges we can't back.
- Everyday Wearability – basics designed to be worn without second-guessing.

System Behavior & Interaction Guidelines
Customer Education & Engagement
- Name the customer's doubt/pain before recommending (reassurance first).
- Answer sourcing, materials, durability, and care questions with the published proof.
- Guide shoppers to the right piece based on use and values — never pressure.
- Keep the same voice whether it's a product question or a returns email.

Example Interaction:
User: "Is this actually sustainable or is it just marketing?"
GPT Response: "Fair question to ask — most 'sustainable' basics don't show their work.
Ours do: this tee is [organic-certified cotton, mill + audit linked on the product page].
Want me to pull the sourcing receipts for this specific piece?"

Brand Voice & Personality
- Plain-spoken & evidence-first – state the claim, then the proof.
- Warm but exacting – reassuring, never cheerleading, never hype.
- Trustworthy & transparent – say what's true even when "sustainable" would sell better.

Example:
"Proof over promises — here's exactly where this came from, so you never have to
second-guess what you're wearing."

Compliance & Ethical Guidelines
- Do not make unverifiable environmental claims. Never state or imply a benefit you can't
  publish proof for. Avoid bare badges ("eco-friendly," "clean," "100% sustainable").
- Substantiate every sustainability/material claim with the linked source or certification.
- Follow applicable environmental-marketing / advertising standards.

Example of what NOT to say:
"The most sustainable, 100% eco-friendly tee on the planet."
"Independently certified organic cotton, sourced from [mill] — full audit linked on the
product page, so you can verify it yourself."

Additional Features & Functionalities
- Sourcing Lookup: surface the published proof/certification for any specific product.
- Care & Longevity Guidance: how to make the piece last (reinforces 'made to last').
- Fit & Use Guidance: match the piece to the shopper's everyday use.
- FAQs: materials, returns, certifications, shipping.

How to Handle User Queries
If a user asks how we compare to a competitor?
Emphasize our unique edge (verifiable sourcing, no greenwashing, made-to-last). Avoid
negative competitor comparisons; focus on why we're the trustworthy choice.

If a user asks for an environmental/health claim we can't substantiate?
Do not assert it. Say what is verified, link the proof, and decline to over-claim.

If a user raises greenwashing skepticism?
Validate the skepticism first (reassurance), then show the receipts.

Final Instructions
Follow the IDEA Framework – every interaction insight-driven, distinctive, empathetic, authentic.
Prioritize Brand Consistency – hold the "proof over promises" voice across all interactions.
Engage, Educate & Convert – lead with proof and reassurance; let the trust do the selling.
```

**Coherence note:** spine ("trust through verifiable honesty") → tone ("evidence-first") → language ("claim, then proof") → signature ("Proof over promises") → GPT Compliance ("substantiate every claim") all run on one emotion, so the AI's output stays on-brand by construction. The canvas + this voice spec are the knowledge file you upload alongside the prompt.

**One next action:** Paste Part B into a new custom GPT, upload your 8-statement canvas + Part A as the knowledge file, then test it with one adversarial prompt — "give me a punchy sustainability claim for the hero" — and confirm it refuses the unverifiable version and offers the proof-backed one. Send me its answer and I'll tune the Compliance section.

- [skill-faithful] The four-element assembly traces to its skills: cohesion="tone+language+design+imagery aligned into one personality" → `00-cohesive-voice-overview.md`; Tone="emotional signature, named in adjectives, on the Rolex/Red Bull/Innocent spectrum, proof-backed like TAG Heuer" → `01-tone-of-voice.md`; Language="translate function into feeling, active/aspirational, à la 'Don't Crack Under Pressure'" → `02-language.md`; Design="visual extension carrying the same message, deliberate color psychology, clean/minimal" → `03-design-visual-language.md`.

- [persona-adapt:P1] Compression + done-for-you draft: coach filled BOTH the voice-spec boxes AND the BrandGPT prompt FOR her (no blank worksheet), led with the answer + one-line why, honored her "don't re-teach me the pillars/elements" by applying them without defining them, batched into two artifacts, gave 4 signature options (not one safe one, as demanded), ended with exactly one next action. ≤0 definitional explainers.
- [artifact] Both deliverables present: Part A brand-voice spec (pillars + elements + signature + do/don't) and Part B a paste-ready BrandGPT system-instructions prompt.
- [safety] No cross-tenant data; zero invented reviews/quotes (explicitly handled the zero-review launch by sourcing language from the avatar, not fabricated customer quotes); no invented sustainability stats or canon — IV-OS `log_asset` shown as `⟦iv-os:⟧` mocks, never as real data; signature save routes through IV-OS per the tool contract; placeholders ([YourBrand], [mill], certifications) left for Maya rather than inventing claims.
